statement.markdown:
Implement a Sudoku Solver using backtracking. However, there seems to be a few bugs in the implementation.

Your task is to identify the mistakes and correct them.

Input:
  - The input will be a 9x9 grid representing the Sudoku board.
  - 0 represents an empty cell. All other numbers in the range 1-9 represent the value in that cell.

Output:
  - Return a boolean indicating whether the given board can be solved or not.
statement.inputFormat: 9x9 grid, where each row is a string of 9 digits (0-9).
statement.outputFormat: Boolean value indicating if the board can be solved or not.
statement.notes: The board is guaranteed to be valid.

constraints.description: The Sudoku board as a 2D array of integers

examples.explanation: This is a valid Sudoku board.

hints.text: First hint text:  Check the logic for checking if a number is valid in a given row or column.

editorial.title: Editorial title:  Mistake-filled Sudoku Solver
editorial.optimalSolution: Optimal solution details:  Check for valid numbers in the correct order
commonMistakes: Mistake 1:  Incorrect order of checking valid numbers
